intitule: Reading Comprehension: The Benefits of Volunteering

enonce.texte:
Read the text below carefully and answer the questions that follow.

The Power of Giving Back

Volunteering is an act of giving one's time and energy to help others without expecting financial reward. In Cameroon, many young people are increasingly involved in community service activities. They participate in environmental clean-up campaigns, teach in rural schools, or assist in local health clinics. The benefits of volunteering are numerous. For the volunteers themselves, it provides an opportunity to develop new skills, gain valuable experience, and build self-confidence. For the community, volunteers are essential in addressing social issues and improving the quality of life for vulnerable populations. Furthermore, volunteering promotes social cohesion and strengthens community bonds. It is also a way for young people to explore career options and demonstrate their commitment to society. In a country where youth unemployment is a significant challenge, volunteering can be a stepping stone to paid employment. Many employers value volunteer experience as it shows initiative, teamwork, and a sense of responsibility.
images: A group of young Cameroonian volunteers in bright t-shirts planting trees in a neighborhood, smiling and working together.

questions.texte: Identify the main idea of the text.
questions.reponse: The main idea is that volunteering provides numerous benefits for both the volunteers themselves and the community.

questions.texte: List three types of volunteering activities mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: Three types are: environmental clean-up campaigns, teaching in rural schools, and assisting in local health clinics.

questions.texte: Explain the benefits of volunteering for the volunteers as described in the text.
questions.reponse: The benefits for volunteers include developing new skills, gaining valuable experience, building self-confidence, and exploring career options.

questions.texte: Analyze why employers value volunteer experience according to the text.
questions.reponse: Employers value volunteer experience because it shows initiative, teamwork, and a sense of responsibility.

questions.texte: Evaluate the role of volunteering in addressing youth unemployment in Cameroon.
questions.reponse: Volunteering can be a stepping stone to paid employment, as it allows young people to gain experience, develop skills, and demonstrate their commitment to society, making them more attractive candidates to employers.

enonce.texte: Match the vocabulary words from the text with their correct definitions.

questions.texte: Match the following words with their definitions.

sousQuestions.reponse: 3. A person who freely offers to take part in an enterprise or undertake a task.
5. A group of people living in the same place or having a particular characteristic in common.
1. An advantage or profit gained from something.
4. The ability to do something well; expertise.
2. Practical contact with and observation of facts or events.

questions.texte: Use the phrase 'community bonds' in a new sentence of your own.
questions.reponse: Volunteering together as a neighborhood can strengthen community bonds and create a more united society. (Accept any grammatically correct sentence that demonstrates understanding of the phrase.)

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following sentences using to + infinitive or in order to to express purpose.

questions.texte: She joined the environmental club. She wanted to help clean the beaches.
questions.reponse: She joined the environmental club to help clean the beaches.

questions.texte: The students organized a fundraising event. They wanted to support the local orphanage.
questions.reponse: The students organized a fundraising event to support the local orphanage.

questions.texte: He volunteered at the hospital. He needed to gain experience in the medical field.
questions.reponse: He volunteered at the hospital in order to gain experience in the medical field.

questions.texte: The government launched a campaign. It wanted to raise awareness about the importance of volunteering.
questions.reponse: The government launched a campaign to raise awareness about the importance of volunteering.

questions.texte: Many young people participate in community service. They want to make a positive difference in their society.
questions.reponse: Many young people participate in community service in order to make a positive difference in their society.

enonce.texte:
You are a student leader at your school. Your principal has asked you to propose a new volunteer initiative to address a pressing issue in your community. Your task is to write a one-page proposal (250-300 words) outlining your initiative. Your proposal must:

  • Define the social issue your initiative will address.
  • Explain the objectives of the initiative.
  • Describe the activities volunteers will carry out.
  • Justify the benefits of the initiative for both the volunteers and the community.
  • Use at least three examples of 'to + infinitive' or 'in order to' to express purpose.
images: A student writing on a whiteboard with a group of other students looking on, planning a project. The board has words like 'Community', 'Action', 'Help'.

questions.texte: Write your proposal below. Be clear, organized, and persuasive.
questions.reponse:
**Exemple de réponse (non-exhaustif):**

Proposal: The 'Clean School, Clean Community' Initiative

1. Introduction
Our community faces a significant challenge with improper waste disposal, which leads to pollution, health hazards, and an unsightly environment. This proposal outlines a volunteer initiative to address this pressing issue.

2. Objectives
Our main objective is to clean up our school and the surrounding neighborhoods in order to create a healthier and more pleasant environment for everyone. We also aim to raise awareness about proper waste management practices and to foster a sense of civic pride among students.

3. Activities
Volunteers will participate in weekly clean-up campaigns, collecting rubbish in designated areas. We will organize educational workshops to teach community members about recycling and waste reduction. Furthermore, we will collaborate with local authorities to place more rubbish bins in strategic locations to prevent littering.

4. Benefits
This initiative will benefit the community by creating a cleaner, safer environment. For the volunteers, it will be an opportunity to develop leadership skills, gain practical experience in project management, and demonstrate their commitment to social responsibility. Through this experience, students will also enhance their CVs in order to improve their future employment prospects.

5. Conclusion
We believe this initiative will have a lasting positive impact on our community. We need the support of the school administration to make this project a success.
